1. A 30-year-old woman presents with a 3-month history of fatigue, joint pain in her hands and wrists, and a butterfly-shaped rash across her cheeks and nasal bridge that worsens with sun exposure. Labs show positive ANA, positive anti-dsDNA antibodies, and low complement levels. Urinalysis shows proteinuria. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
Systemic lupus erythematosus
Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is a systemic autoimmune disease characterized by the production of autoantibodies against nuclear antigens (ANA, anti-dsDNA). Clinical manifestations include the classic malar butterfly rash, photosensitivity, arthritis, nephritis, and hematologic abnormalities. Positive ANA is highly sensitive but not specific; anti-dsDNA antibodies are highly specific for SLE.
